subject: 🏃 Thursday — Easy 4 mi (but we need to talk about yesterday)
html:
Today's workout: Easy run 4 mi — soft surface (Sawyer Camp or Bayfront), 9:00–9:30/mi, HR under 140. But before you lace up, I need to flag something.

Yesterday's Strava shows 5.79 mi at 8:13/mi avg, 148 bpm heart rate. That was supposed to be Mobility Deep-Dive day — no running. You exceeded the Chassis Build distance cap (5 mi max), ran well above the HR ceiling (140), and your splits included 7:40 and 7:33 pushes that are bordering on tempo. More importantly, you skipped the mobility session that directly targets the hip IR deficits driving your knee issue. The chassis work isn't optional filler — it's the reason this phase exists.

I need your knee status from yesterday before you run today. If it's Green, do today's 4 mi but genuinely easy — think 9:30/mi and let the watch feel boring. If it's Yellow, swap to cycling and double today's mobility. If it's Red, full stop and we talk to the PT.

After the run: Try to make up the missed mobility — at minimum the foam roll, 90/90 flow with forward fold, and banded hip distraction. Even 15 minutes matters.

Focus cue: HR under 140, full stop. If it creeps up, walk until it drops. The aerobic base will get built in Weeks 5–14 — right now, discipline at easy pace is the workout.

Where you are: Week 2 of 4 in Chassis Build, 15 days to PT follow-up on May 15. The chassis work IS the training right now. The miles are just maintenance.
